videos.title: WATCH THIS BEFORE GOING TO THE BALTIC CAPITALS! (TALLINN, RIGA, & VILNIUS)
videos.description:
We had an epic time road tripping through the Baltic capitals of Vilnius, Riga, and Tallinn.  Our week long Baltic road trip includes our favorite sights in each of the three Baltic countries of Lithuania, Latvia, and Estonia.  We also give our recommendations for must try Baltic food.

After making our way from Poland, we entered our first Baltic country Lithuania to explore its capital of Vilnius.  The Old Town of Vilnius is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and home to our first must see sight, Cathedral Square.  We also recommend visiting Literary Street, and the Republic of Užupis.  Our recommendations for Lithuanian cuisine are Leičiai for Vilnius mead and local beer, and Loky's for Lithuanian beetroot soup and boiled potato zeppelins.  

⬇️ Check out our full walking tour of Vilnius, Lithuania https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GJfPjAnGu88&t=2s

After leaving Lithuania, the next Baltic country on our road trip was Latvia.  It was only a 3 1/2 hour drive from Vilnius to the Latvian capital of Riga.  Our first must see sight in Riga is the House of the Blackheads.  We also recommend seeing the Cat House, and visiting the Black Magic Bar to try Riga Black Balsam.  For Latvian cuisine, our recommendation is Key to Riga. We recommend trying the Latvian national dish (grey peas with bacon) with a local beer. 

⬇️ Check out our full walking tour of Riga, Latvia
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=FIK-Ak4ErOE

The northernmost and final Baltic state we visited was Estonia.  It’s about a 4 hour drive from Riga, Latvia to Estonia’s Capital of Tallinn. Our first must see sight in Tallinn, Estonia is Toompea Hill in the Old Town. Toompea Hill is home to Toompea Castle and Estonia’s parliament building. We also recommend the Danish King’s Garden where you can see the well-preserved medieval wall.  Finally, visit the hipster area of Kalamaja.  To experience medieval dining in Tallinn, we recommend the Third Dragon and Olde Hansa.  

⬇️ Check out our full walking tour of Tallinn, Estonia
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=E_0Ea1iTgfE&t=242s

videos.transcript: Welcome to my 2 Days Vilnius Itinerary, where I’ll take you through the must-visit landmarks, hidden gems, and unforgettable experiences in this charming city! Don’t forget to like, comment, and share this video, and subscribe to my channel for more travel adventures. If you need more information or have any questions, feel free to ask me in the comments below—I’d love to help! We landed at Riga Airport after a flight from Larnaca Airport. After arriving in Vilnius, we checked in at the Panorama Hotel, which offered stunning views of the city. Then, we started our exploration by heading toward the old town, eager to dive into its charm. Our first major stop was the Gates of Dawn, a historic and spiritual landmark of Vilnius. We visited the Church of St. Theresa, a beautiful example of Baroque architecture. Next, we admired the Orthodox Church of the Holy Spirit, known for its vibrant interior. Continuing our walk, we headed down Aušros Vartai street, soaking in the lively atmosphere. We stopped at the Holy Trinity Uniate Church, a unique blend of cultural and religious influences. Our journey brought us to Dr. Jono Basanavičiaus aikštė, a bustling square filled with energy. We took a moment to appreciate the Chapel of Saint Casimir, dedicated to Lithuania's patron saint. Then, we arrived at Town Hall Square, a central meeting point and historical area of Vilnius. The square itself was vibrant and full of life, surrounded by stunning architecture. We explored Vilnius Town Hall, an iconic building with centuries of history. Nearby, we admired the Town Hall Square Fountain & Compass, a modern yet artistic touch to the area. Next, we visited St. Nicholas' Church, the oldest and one of the most serene churches in Vilnius. We also stopped at Saint Parasceve Orthodox Church, rich with Orthodox Christian traditions. Pilies Street was a delightful experience, lined with shops, cafes, and a lively atmosphere. Throughout our walk, the decorated walls added a layer of artistic charm, showcasing Vilnius' creative spirit. Then we headed to Cathedral Square, the heart of Vilnius' historical district. Cathedral Square was bustling with locals and tourists, creating a lively atmosphere. The open space was surrounded by stunning architecture and held a rich sense of history. We stopped to admire the Monument to Grand Duke Gediminas, a symbol of Lithuania’s past. Next, we explored the Palace of the Grand Dukes of Lithuania, which showcases the grandeur of former times. Vilnius Cathedral stood majestically, captivating us with its neoclassical design. Inside the cathedral, the intricate details and sacred atmosphere left us in awe. The Bell Tower of Vilnius Cathedral offered a unique perspective of the square and beyond. Walking along Gedimino Street, we enjoyed the vibrant shops and historic buildings. We paused at a cozy café to enjoy some coffee and take in the city’s charm. After that, we headed toward the funicular ride leading to Gediminas Hill. On the way, we passed the New Arsenal of the National Museum of Lithuania, a remarkable sight. The Funicular to Gediminas Hill was a fun and scenic way to ascend. Once at Gediminas Hill, the panoramic views of Vilnius were absolutely breathtaking. We took our time exploring the lush greenery and historic significance of the hill. At Gediminas Castle Tower, we marveled at its history and the stories it holds. Nearby, the Ducal Palace of the Upper Castle offered more insights into Lithuania’s medieval past. The serene flow of the Neris River added a calming backdrop to our exploration. We crossed Tiltas per Vilnią, a charming bridge connecting key parts of the area. The Three Crosses Monument stood proudly on a hill, offering stunning views of the city. Its historical and cultural importance made it one of the most memorable spots of our visit. Then we completed our first day of exploration with full of pleasure, feeling accomplished and inspired. We headed back to the hotel to rest and recharge for the next day's itinerary. The next day, we started by heading to the old town again, eager to explore more. Our first stop was the beautiful Bernardine Garden, a lush and tranquil escape in the city. We took a peaceful walk along the garden, enjoying the well-maintained pathways. The scenic views and serene ambiance of the garden made it a perfect start to the day. We admired the riverside scenery, surrounded by nature, and watched the ducks swimming gracefully. The Bernardine Garden Musical Fountain added a playful and vibrant touch to the surroundings. Next, we visited St. Anne's Church, a Gothic masterpiece with intricate details. Inside St. Anne's Church, the serene atmosphere and architecture left us in awe. We stopped at the Monument to Adam Mickiewicz, honoring the famous poet. Nearby, we admired the Neofreska „Bernardinas Sienietis,” a striking modern fresco. We wandered along Literary Street, known for its artistic plaques and literary connections. The Church of St. Michael stood out with its unique Baroque and Renaissance features. Crossing the Bridge of Užupis, we entered the quirky and artistic Užupis district. The district's bohemian vibe and creative spirit made it one of the most fascinating places we visited. We read the Constitution of the Republic of Užupis, an imaginative and inspiring declaration. The Angel of Užupis statue stood proudly, symbolizing the district's artistic soul. Strolling along Uzupio Street, we explored its galleries and unique shops. We visited Skersvėjis, a artistic and cozy spot in Užupis. At Užupio skveras, we relaxed in the small park and enjoyed the creative installations. The Užupis Cat, a playful sculpture, added a whimsical touch to the district. Then we headed to the upper side of the city for more exploration. The Missionary Gardens offered stunning views and a peaceful retreat. At the Bastion of the Vilnius Defensive Wall, we learned about the city’s fortifications and history. From its vantage point, the panoramic views of Vilnius were spectacular and unforgettable. We strolled along Šventosios Dvasios gatvė, a charming street full of history and character. In the evening, we made our way to the famous Trakai Castle, a must-visit destination. The distance from Vilnius to Trakai Castle was short, making it a convenient side trip. Surrounded by a serene lake and picturesque landscapes, the castle was breathtaking. Exploring Trakai Castle, we delved into its fascinating history and unique architecture. We completed our second and last day with full of satisfaction, feeling grateful for the experiences. Finally, we returned to the hotel to rest and prepare for our flight the next morning. When planning your 2-day Vilnius itinerary, keep in mind that walking or using public transportation is the best way to explore the city, as it’s compact and pedestrian-friendly. Immerse yourself in the local culture by visiting the historical sites and engaging with locals, who are friendly and often speak English. The currency used in Vilnius is the Euro, so it’s convenient for travelers from Europe, and you’ll find plenty of ATMs and exchange options. Don’t miss trying local dishes like cepelinai (potato dumplings) and šaltibarščiai (cold beet soup) for an authentic Lithuanian culinary experience. For shopping, head to Pilies Street or local markets to find unique souvenirs, including amber jewelry and traditional crafts. Accommodation options range from budget-friendly hostels to luxurious hotels—staying near the old town will save you time and make sightseeing easier. The best time to visit Vilnius is during late spring or early autumn when the weather is pleasant and outdoor attractions are at their best. Lastly, plan your days well to make the most of your visit, but leave some flexibility to discover hidden gems along the way! Thank you for joining me on this 2 Days Vilnius Itinerary—I hope it inspires your own adventure in this beautiful city! Don’t forget to like, comment, and share this video, and subscribe to my channel for more travel guides and tips. If you have any questions or need more information, feel free to ask me in the comments—I’m happy to help!

videos.transcript: Hello and welcome to our brand new vlog, where we're about to uncover the hidden gems and captivating beauty of Vilnius, Lithuania’s historic capital. From the cobblestone charm of the Old Town to the artistic enclave of Užupis, we’re taking you on a journey through its enchanting streets, majestic churches, and vibrant local life. We'll also dive into the city's rich history, explore its modern cultural scene, and even taste some traditional Lithuanian delicacies. Whether you're a history buff, a foodie, or simply in love with travel, there's something in Vilnius for everyone. Don't forget to subscribe to our channel for more exciting travel vlogs and to join us as we explore the wonders of Vilnius together! Vilnius is the capital city of Lithuania, known for its rich history and architectural variety, blending medieval, neoclassical, and modern styles. It is the largest city in the country and serves as an important cultural, economic, and political center. Vilnius is famous for its Old Town, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, and is home to numerous historical landmarks, vibrant cultural scenes, and educational institutions. The city is characterized by its diverse population and a mix of different cultures, making it a melting pot of ideas and traditions. Vilnius is also noted for its green spaces, offering a blend of urban and natural environments. To get to Vilnius, travelers have several options, including flying into Vilnius International Airport, the main air gateway to the country. The airport is well-connected with major cities across Europe and some long-haul destinations. Alternatively, visitors can travel to Vilnius by train, with direct services from neighboring countries like Poland, Belarus, and Russia. Buses are another popular option, offering extensive routes that connect Vilnius with other European cities. For those preferring to drive, the city is accessible via well-maintained highways that link it with other major urban centers in the region. Traveling around Vilnius can be done conveniently through various modes of transportation. The city has an efficient public transportation system, including buses and trolleybuses that cover most areas of the city. For more personalized travel, taxis and ride-sharing services are readily available. Vilnius also promotes sustainable travel, with an expanding network of bike lanes and bike-sharing programs, making cycling a viable option for getting around. Additionally, the city's compact size and pedestrian-friendly streets make walking a pleasant and often preferred way to explore, especially in the Old Town area. Using public transport in Vilnius is a convenient and efficient way to navigate the city. The system comprises buses, trolleybuses, and a recently introduced tram line, covering most areas of the city and its suburbs. Tickets can be purchased at kiosks, from ticket machines at major stops, or directly from the driver, and they must be validated upon boarding. Visitors can choose from single-ride tickets or various pass options, such as daily, weekly, or monthly passes, which offer unlimited travel and are more economical for longer stays. Additionally, real-time information about routes, schedules, and ticket prices is available through mobile apps and online platforms, making it easier for travelers to plan their journeys. Vilnius offers a variety of attractions for visitors to see. The Old Town, with its narrow streets and historic buildings, is a must-visit, showcasing the city's medieval past. Key landmarks include the Vilnius Cathedral, Gediminas Tower, and the Presidential Palace. Art enthusiasts will enjoy the Contemporary Art Centre and the National Museum of Lithuania. For a taste of local life, the Užupis district, known for its bohemian atmosphere and artistic community, is a unique area to explore. Additionally, the city's parks, such as Vingis Park, provide a scenic retreat, while the numerous cafes and restaurants offer a chance to sample Lithuanian cuisine. In Vilnius, a funicular ride to Gediminas Hill offers a unique and picturesque way to ascend, providing visitors with stunning views of the city below. Once at the top, Gediminas Castle Tower awaits, standing as a testament to Lithuania’s medieval past and offering panoramic vistas that are simply breathtaking. Not far from here, the Gates of Dawn, the city’s famous gateway, houses a revered icon of the Virgin Mary, making it a significant spiritual site. Vilnius Cathedral, with its grand neoclassical façade, dominates the city's skyline and is a must-visit for its architectural beauty and historical importance. Nearby, the Palace of the Grand Dukes of Lithuania invites explorers to delve into the country’s regal history through its extensive exhibitions. Bernardine Garden provides a tranquil escape with its lush greenery and peaceful paths alongside the Vilnia River. Town Hall Square, at the heart of the Old Town, buzzes with life, surrounded by historic buildings and lively cafes. The Three Crosses Monument, perched on a hill, offers not only a historical narrative but also a magnificent view of the city, especially at sunset. Cathedral Square serves as a focal point for locals and tourists alike, with its open space and backdrop of significant landmarks. St. Anne’s Church impresses visitors with its intricate Gothic architecture, a masterpiece of craftsmanship and devotion. Literary Street celebrates the city's rich literary tradition, with plaques honoring both Lithuanian and international writers. Vilnius Town Hall, an emblem of civic pride, hosts various cultural events and exhibitions throughout the year. Uzupio Street, in the heart of the bohemian Užupis district, showcases the area’s artistic flair and unconventional spirit. The Constitution of the Republic of Užupis, displayed publicly, underscores the community’s whimsical and free-spirited ethos. Lastly, the Bastion of the Vilnius Defensive Wall, a reminder of the city’s fortifications, offers visitors a glimpse into its defensive past, complete with a museum and scenic viewpoints. Each of these 15 destinations provides a unique insight into Vilnius’s rich tapestry of history, culture, and beauty, making the city a captivating place to explore. The best time to visit Vilnius is during the late spring (May to June) and early autumn (September to October) months. During these periods, the weather is mild and pleasant, ideal for exploring the city's outdoor attractions and historical sites. Summer (July to August) is also a popular time due to its warm temperatures and numerous festivals, but it can be more crowded and expensive. Winter (December to February) offers a different charm with its snowy landscapes and Christmas markets, although it can be quite cold. Each season in Vilnius presents a unique experience, making it a year-round destination depending on visitors’ interests. Prices in Vilnius are generally considered affordable compared to other European capitals. Dining out in local restaurants and cafes can be quite economical, with a variety of options to suit different budgets. Accommodation prices vary, with options ranging from budget-friendly hostels to luxurious hotels, catering to all types of travelers. Public transportation is reasonably priced, making it an efficient and cost-effective way to get around the city. Tourist attractions, such as museums and historical sites, often have modest entrance fees, and there are many free activities and sights to enjoy as well. Vilnius offers a diverse culinary scene that blends traditional Lithuanian cuisine with modern influences. Visitors should try local dishes such as Cepelinai (potato dumplings stuffed with meat or cheese), Šaltibarščiai (cold beetroot soup), and Kibinai (traditional pastries filled with meat). For a sweet treat, Sakotis, a tree-like cake, is a popular choice. The city’s growing food scene also includes international cuisines, vegan and vegetarian options, and trendy cafes serving local coffee and pastries. Additionally, Vilnius hosts food markets and festivals where one can sample a variety of dishes and local produce. Shopping in Vilnius offers a mix of traditional and modern options. Visitors can buy authentic Lithuanian souvenirs such as amber jewelry, linen products, and handcrafted wooden items, which reflect the country's cultural heritage. The Old Town is a great place to find these traditional items, along with art galleries selling local artworks. For more contemporary shopping, there are several shopping malls and boutiques throughout the city that offer fashion, electronics, and international brands. Additionally, local markets and fairs are excellent for finding unique gifts and hand-made crafts. Vilnius offers a wide range of accommodation options to suit different preferences and budgets. For those seeking luxury, there are high-end hotels in the city center, offering premium amenities and close proximity to major attractions. Mid-range hotels and boutique accommodations provide comfortable stays with a touch of local charm. Budget travelers can find numerous hostels and guesthouses, especially around the Old Town, offering affordable and sociable lodging. For a more immersive experience, apartment rentals are available, allowing visitors to live like locals. The city's accommodation is generally well-located, providing easy access to public transportation and key tourist spots. Vilnius is generally safe for visitors, with crime rates lower than many other European cities. Like any urban area, it's advisable to take standard safety precautions, such as being aware of your surroundings, especially at night, and keeping valuables secure. The city center and tourist areas are well-patrolled and secure, but it's wise to be cautious in less crowded or poorly lit areas. Petty crimes like pickpocketing can occur in busy tourist spots, so it's important to stay vigilant. Overall, with a friendly and helpful local population, Vilnius provides a welcoming and secure environment for travelers. Vilnius is definitely worth visiting for its unique blend of historical charm and contemporary culture. The city's Old Town, a UNESCO World Heritage site, offers a fascinating glimpse into its medieval past with well-preserved buildings and cobblestone streets. Vilnius is also known for its vibrant arts scene, numerous museums, and galleries that showcase both traditional and modern works. The city's culinary landscape is diverse, offering a mix of traditional Lithuanian cuisine and international flavors. Additionally, Vilnius’s green spaces and parks provide a peaceful escape from the urban environment. The city's combination of history, culture, and natural beauty makes it an enriching and worthwhile destination. As we wrap up our incredible journey through Vilnius, we hope you've been captivated by its historical beauty, vibrant culture, and the warmth of its people as much as we have. From the ancient walls of Gediminas Castle to the quirky streets of Užupis, Vilnius has proven to be a city of surprises and delights at every turn. We're grateful you joined us on this adventure, exploring Lithuania's charming capital. If you enjoyed this vlog and want to keep up with our future travels, make sure to hit that subscribe button. Thank you for watching, and we can't wait to share our next destination with you!

Starting with number one, Vnius Oldtown. Vnius Oldtown, a UNESCO World Heritage site, is the heart and soul of the city. Known for its medieval charm, it features cobblestone streets, grand churches, and historic buildings. Among the must-sea landmarks are St. An's Church, the presidential palace, and Py Street lined with shops and cafes. Visitors can immerse themselves in the city's rich history while enjoying a lively, vibrant atmosphere. The old town offers an exceptional blend of culture, architecture, and local traditions, making it the top destination in [Music] Vnius. Moving on to number two, Jedima's Tower. Jedimina's Tower is an iconic symbol of Vnius and part of the Vilmas Castle complex. The tower sits on a hill offering spectacular panoramic views of the city. Visitors can climb to the top for breathtaking views of the old town, the nearest river, and the city's surrounding areas. The tower's historical significance dates back to the 14th century when it was a key part of the defensive [Music] system. Number three, Vilius TV Tower. Vnius TV tower stands tall as one of the tallest structures in Lithuania. offering spectacular panoramic views of the city. At the top, visitors can enjoy a rotating restaurant with a 360 degree view of Vnius and the surrounding landscape. The tower was originally built for broadcasting purposes, but has since become a popular tourist attraction. It provides visitors with an opportunity to appreciate the city's layout and its historical landmarks from above. For those looking to see Vnius from a unique perspective, the Vilnius TV Tower offers an unforgettable [Music] experience. Number four, Museum of Occupations and Freedom Fights. The Museum of Occupations and Freedom Fights, housed in the former KGB headquarters, provides a poignant and powerful look into Lithuania's history during Soviet occupation. The museum chronicles Lithuania's struggle for independence through exhibits of photographs, documents, and personal stories. Visitors can tour the rooms that once held political prisoners and learn about the harsh realities of the Soviet era. The museum also highlights Lithuania's freedom movement and eventual independence, offering a deeply emotional and informative experience for anyone interested in the nation's history and resilience. [Music] Number five in our list, Vengeis [Music] Park. Vengeis Park is a spacious, lush green park located along the nearest river. Perfect for a day of relaxation or outdoor activities. This expansive park is the largest in Vilmius, offering plenty of walking paths, sports fields, and peaceful picnic spots. It's a popular location for locals and tourists alike, especially in summer. The park also hosts concerts and outdoor events, drawing large crowds. Whether you're jogging, cycling, or simply enjoying nature, Visi Park provides a serene escape from the hustle and bustle of the [Music] city. Number six, Acropolis Vnius. Acropolis Vnius is a massive shopping and entertainment complex offering a wide range of retail stores, restaurants, and attractions. It's one of the largest malls in the Baltics, providing everything from high-end brands to local shops. Visitors can enjoy activities such as ice skating and bowling, making it a family-friendly destination. Acropolis also features an impressive cinema, offering the latest films in a comfortable setting. Whether you're shopping, dining, or simply enjoying the entertainment, Acropolis is a must- vvisit spot for those seeking a dynamic experience in [Music] Vnius. Number seven, Lithuanian National Museum. The Lithuanian National Museum located near the castle hill offers a comprehensive journey through the country's history and culture with exhibits ranging from prehistoric artifacts to medieval history. The museum provides a deep dive into Lithuania's rich past. It also explores the country's customs, traditions, and significant events such as the struggle for independence. The museum is an essential stop for those seeking a deeper understanding of Lithuanian heritage. Its educational and engaging exhibits make it an enriching experience for both locals and tourists alike. Number eight, Vilmius University. Vnius University, founded in 1579 is one of the oldest and most prestigious universities in Eastern Europe. Its beautiful campus features a mix of Renaissance, Baroque, and classical architectural styles. The university is renowned for its academic excellence and vibrant student life. Visitors can explore its historic buildings, peaceful courtyards, and libraries. The university's library is home to valuable manuscripts and is a cultural hub for the city. Vnius University is a key educational institution contributing greatly to the city's intellectual and cultural [Music] landscape. Number nine, Zermini Aqua [Music] Park. The park features multiple pools, water slides, and spa facilities, making it perfect for a day of aquatic enjoyment. Families can enjoy the shallow pools designed for children, while thrillsekers can test the exhilarating slides. The park also has a wellness area for those seeking a more relaxing experience. Whether you're looking for high energy fun or a peaceful retreat, Surinai Aqua Park provides an exciting escape for all. [Music] Finally, number 10, Seaman's Arena. Seaman's Arena is the premier sports and entertainment venue in Vnius, hosting major concerts, sports events, and cultural shows. The arena has a seating capacity of over 10,000, making it a central hub for large-scale events. It's the home of the Vius basketball team and hosts international sporting competitions. There are countless things to do in Vilnius, making it an ideal destination for travelers seeking a unique experience.

One of the top things to do in Vilnius is to explore its UNESCO-listed Old Town. Wandering through the cobblestone streets, you'll discover charming architecture, quaint cafes, and numerous historical landmarks. The things to do in Vilnius Old Town are endless, from visiting the stunning St. Anne's Church to admiring the grand Vilnius Cathedral.

For those interested in history, the top 10 things to do in Vilnius should include a visit to the Gate of Dawn, a significant religious and cultural site. The Museum of Occupations and Freedom Fights, housed in a former KGB building, offers a sobering look at Lithuania's tumultuous past.

Art enthusiasts will find plenty of cool things to do in Vilnius, such as exploring the bohemian district of Užupis, known for its quirky street art and independent spirit. The Contemporary Art Centre showcases cutting-edge exhibitions, making it one of the best things to do in Vilnius for culture lovers.

Nature lovers can enjoy free things to do in Vilnius by strolling through the picturesque Bernardine Gardens or climbing the Hill of Three Crosses for panoramic views of the city. These activities are particularly enjoyable during the warmer months, but there are also plenty of things to do in Vilnius in winter, such as ice skating in the city center or warming up in cozy Lithuanian restaurants.

For those seeking unusual things to do in Vilnius, consider taking a hot air balloon ride over the city or visiting the quirky Museum of Illusions. These experiences offer a unique perspective on the Lithuanian capital.

As night falls, the fun things to do in Vilnius continue. The city's nightlife scene is vibrant, with numerous bars, clubs, and live music venues. Exploring the lively Pilies Street or enjoying a cocktail at a rooftop bar are popular things to do in Vilnius at night.

Food lovers will delight in the culinary scene, which is an integral part of any Vilnius travel guide. Trying traditional Lithuanian dishes like cepelinai or šaltibarščiai should be on every visitor's list of top ten things to do in Vilnius.

Beyond the city limits, there are numerous things to do in Lithuania Vilnius serves as an excellent base for day trips to nearby attractions like Trakai Island Castle or the Hill of Crosses.

Whether you're interested in history, art, nature, or culinary experiences, Vilnius offers something for everyone. This charming Baltic capital continues to surprise and delight visitors with its diverse array of attractions and activities, making it a must-visit destination in Eastern Europe.

videos.transcript: Vius, the capital city of Lithuania, is best known for its historical charm and particularly its UNESCO listed old town. It is located in the southeast part of Lithuania and is the second largest city in the Baltic states with winding cobbled streets, charming local markets, beautiful churches and palaces along with modern additions. This city is a fantastic blend of old and new, and provides a brilliant array of attractions. Before we get into the top 10 things to do in Vius, we've included links in the description to various discount codes and links to resources of things to do, so make sure you check those out. At number 10 is the Vius Television Tower. The Vnius Television Tower is the tallest building in Lithuania at 326.5 m high and is an immensely recognizable fixture on the cityscape. Located in Vius's northeastern district, the television tower played host to the January events that took place in January 1991 as Lithuania fought for its independence. Today, visitors can go up to a revolving circular observation platform which provides spectacular views across the whole city. Number nine is the hop- on hopoff bus Vius. There's a lot to like about Vnius, and visitors can see it all with a 24 or 48 hour ticket for the hop- on hop off bus, Vnius. There's Wi-Fi on board, plus multilingual audio commentary to explain exactly what you're looking at as you cruise between the important sites and monuments. Hop off at any point on your tour and rejoin at your leisure. With panoramic views from the bus, Lithuania's capital has never been so easy to explore. At number four is Via's Cathedral. The Cathedral Basilica of St. Donislaus and St. Lisau of Vius often goes by the much shorter name Vius Cathedral. It's located just off Cathedral Square in Vius Oldtown. Visitors can admire the decor and authentic construction and then climb to the top of the 13th century tower and take in the Barack architecture and cobblestone streets from up high or see Vnius from a virtual lookout platform instead. Number seven is the Getty Tower. Getty castle tower is part of what would have been called the upper castle, a wider complex built in the 14th century in Vnius. It has an interesting hexagonal shape and houses a weapons and armory exhibition inside. The tower can be reached by either walking up the castle hill or visitors can take the scenic finicular. An observation deck on the top of the tower is the best place from which to appreciate a magnificent panorama of Vius. At number six is Europos Parkas. Europos Parkas is an outdoor wooded wonderland located 17 km from Vnius. It was founded in 1991 by Lithuanian sculptor Gintaras Karosas and features over 90 sculptures and art installations spread over 55 hectares with works originating from 27 different countries. Visitors can see the breathtaking hilly pastures, atmospheric forest trails, and freshwater springs on the hunt for thought-provoking sculptures and towering art installations that spring up from the landscape, making an atmospheric adventure of art, nature, and imagination. Number five is Lubavas Manor Museum. Located a short 30-minute drive from Vius, Lubavas Manor is one of Lithuania's oldest estates. Visitors can amble around the scenic grounds and tour the manor and learn about the amazing history and the tales of intrigue hidden within. Visit the beautifully restored watermill grinding flour out of grain and learn the history of the tradition. Lubis Manor Museum is a slice of Lithuanian life that preserves centuries of traditional heritage. At number four is St. Anne's Church. Standing tall above the Lithuanian capital of Vnius, St. Ants Church was built for Anna, Grand Duchess of Lithuania, and has watched history unfold around it. The 500-year-old church remains a prime example of Gothic architecture and is one of the prettiest buildings in Lithuania. Visitors can admire its picturesque brick towers and rose windows combined with a beautiful interior. Number three is Vingus Park. Vingus Park is by far the largest park in Vius, spanning 400 acres. With a small botanical garden, sports complex, outdoor concert venue, several playgrounds, and plenty of benches to sit on, visitors can find some active or inactive fun. Numerous cycling and running trails weave throughout the park, and cyclists have the option to rent a bike. At number two, wander around Vnia's Oldtown. VNA's old town is a UNESCO World Heritage site and sits at the point where two of Lithuania's main rivers converge. It is known for being one of the premium medieval towns in Europe thanks to its amazingly wellpreserved buildings and showcases architecture in the Gothic, Renaissance and neocclassical styles. All the structures are centered on a main piaza and visitors will find cobbled streets that are dotted with bars and eeries. And at number one is tour to track island and track Castle. Take a trip from Vnius and venture into the Lithuanian countryside on a historic sightseeing adventure. Marvel at the magnificent of Trackey Castle, standing proud surrounded by beautiful wetlands. Then take a tour of its enchanting interior. The fairy tale Trackey Castle, which sits pretty in the middle of a stunning lake, has many rooms and chambers to explore and a gripping history to uncover. So, that sums up our top 10 things to do in Vnius. We hope you enjoyed. If you did, please leave a like on the video. And if you're new here, feel free to subscribe. Until next video, have a great day.
